text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Amelia Hart  
St. Cuthbert’s Hospital  
Department of Neurology, 12 Mill Lane, Manchester M2 5LA  

Dear Dr Amelia Hart,  

Alex Turner, DOB: 23-02-1991, Hospital No: M438210 NHS No. 9057314826  
Flat 3, 18 Brook Street, Manchester, M1 7DG 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures (first event 2019; most recent 14 August 2025)  
Neuroimaging: Brain MRI (March 2024) – no structural abnormality  
EEG (April 2024) – non-specific; no unequivocal epileptiform discharges 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
Midazolam 10 mg buccal as rescue, supplied for prolonged events 

Plan: 

Continue Levetiracetam at current dose  
Maintain seizure diary; integrate smartwatch fall-detection alerts as time markers  
Safety advice reinforced; no driving in accordance with DVLA guidance  
Follow-up in 6 months, sooner if breakthrough clusters occur 

I reviewed Alex Turner in clinic today. Since our last appointment in February 2025, they describe brief loss-of-awareness episodes and occasional collapses. Based on their account and corroborating smartwatch fall-detection alerts, events appear to occur roughly every three to four months. The most recent episode was on 14 August 2025 at approximately 07:10, with a fall alert logged on the device and a brief post-event confusion of about 10 minutes. There were no clear tongue bites reported and only mild myalgia afterwards. 

They report good adherence to Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily, though they missed an evening dose two days prior to the August event. Sleep has been irregular due to shift work; alcohol intake is minimal. There is no consistent aura, and witnesses describe a sudden pause with a subsequent drop on two occasions this year; the smartwatch notifications have been helpful in reconstructing timing, particularly for unwitnessed morning events. 

Examination today is unremarkable. Review of prior investigations shows a structurally normal MRI and a non-specific EEG without definite epileptiform activity. Given the ongoing events at an every-few-months cadence and the recent dose omission, I have recommended continuing Levetiracetam at the present dose while we prioritise sleep regularity and dose adherence. I have asked Alex Turner to maintain a detailed seizure diary and to export the smartwatch fall-detection timestamps to align with symptom entries; this should aid in clarifying patterns and potential triggers. Rescue buccal midazolam remains available for prolonged events exceeding five minutes. 

We revisited safety measures: shower rather than bath, avoid heights and open water without supervision, and ensure kitchen precautions. In line with DVLA guidance, they should not drive and should notify the DVLA if not already done. 

I will review them again in six months. If the pattern accelerates or if there are clusters, please contact our service sooner for earlier review. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: events appear to occur roughly every three to four months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the events occur "roughly every three to four months," which implies approximately 1 seizure per 3 to 4 months. The most recent event was on 14 August 2025, so the patient is not seizure-free for 6 months. No other competing frequencies or clusters are provided. Therefore, normalize to 1 per 3 to 4 month.
seizure_frequency_number: events appear to occur roughly every three to four months

flaws.description: Gold (1 per 3 to 4 month) disagrees with Reference (1 per 3 month)
